steps.title: Substitute and apply the given condition
steps.content:
We have $g(x) = b - 15x$. Substituting $x = c+7$:
$$g(c+7) = b - 15(c+7) = b - 15c - 105$$
We're told that $g(c+7) = \frac{c}{4}$, so:
$$b - 15c - 105 = \frac{c}{4}$$

steps.title: Solve for b and simplify
steps.content:
Add $15c + 105$ to both sides:
$$b = \frac{c}{4} + 15c + 105$$
Convert to a common denominator: $15c = \frac{60c}{4}$:
$$b = \frac{c}{4} + \frac{60c}{4} + 105 = \frac{61c}{4} + 105$$
That matches choice C — C is correct.
